summary: The test suite demonstrates solid coverage of core requirements: glob pattern conversion/matching, label deduplication, priority ordering, multiple labels per file, and CLI integration. Unit tests (LabelAssigner.Tests.ps1) are well-structured with clear Describe blocks and good happy-path coverage. Integration tests (Run-ActTests.ps1) appropriately validate the GitHub Actions workflow via act, actionlint, and workflow structure checks. However, rigor gaps exist: limited error scenario testing (only file-not-found), no tests for malformed JSON rules, missing tests for edge cases like special characters, whitespace handling, null/empty rule fields, and invalid priorities. The test suite would catch regressions in core functionality but may miss input validation failures and unusual edge cases. Design is strong—excellent organization, good fixture patterns, proper isolation with temp directories—though Run-ActTests.ps1 is complex and relies on external fixture files (fixtures/case-*.txt, rules.json, .actrc, workflow YAML) whose content is not shown, creating uncertainty about full executability.
